name: CodeGraph
description:
CodeGraph is a local-first code intelligence library, CLI, and MCP server. It parses any supported codebase with tree-sitter, stores symbols, edges, and files in an embedded SQLite database (WAL + FTS5), and exposes the resulting knowledge graph to AI coding agents over the Model Context Protocol. Extraction is deterministic — derived from the AST, never LLM-summarized — so the graph is a fast, cached substitute for the grep-and-read loop an agent would otherwise run.

The product ships as a single npm package (@colbymchenry/codegraph) that serves three roles from one binary: a multi-agent installer, an indexer, and a long-lived MCP server. Per-project data lives entirely on the developer's machine in a .codegraph/ directory; there is no backend service in the request path. The only outbound network traffic is anonymous, opt-out usage telemetry.

Two actors drive the system: developers, who install it and run CLI commands, and AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, opencode), which query the graph over MCP to answer structural and flow questions in a few fast calls instead of dozens of file reads.

The repository also contains two supporting deployables: an Astro/Starlight documentation site and a Cloudflare Worker that ingests anonymous telemetry and forwards it to PostHog.
